answers: DeFi (Decentralized Finance) = financial services on blockchain without banks. Lending, borrowing, trading — all peer-to-peer.
DeFi examples: Uniswap (trading), Aave (lending), Compound (interest), and stablecoins (USDC, DAI).
Benefits: no intermediaries, global access, transparent, programmable money. Risks: smart contract bugs, volatility, scams.
DeFi basics: you need a crypto wallet (MetaMask), some crypto, and access to DeFi protocols. Start small to learn.

feelings.question: defi decentralized finance ki?
feelings.answer: Defi (decentralized finance) is a financial services platform on blockchain that enables peer-to-peer transactions without banks or intermediaries, making it more secure and efficient than traditional banking systems.
